title: Ending of original series
wikitext:
[]: Ending of original series             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:19 pm I apologize if there is already a thread, Im new, so you can laugh lol. In the orig. series, not end of eva which ruled, just what in the HELL were they trying to pass over our eyes in ep. 25/26 of the orig.????? Some psychiatric simulator??? I mean come on! I almost wished I hadn't watched the last 2 eps ( i re-watch the series from time to time. ). Did anything actually occur in those last two? I mean they even didnt animate half of it, with shitty marker art .....

[]: Re: Ending of original series             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:26 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I apologize if there is already a thread, Im new, so you can laugh lol. In the orig. series, not end of eva which ruled, just what in the HELL were they trying to pass over our eyes in ep. 25/26 of the orig.????? Some psychiatric simulator??? I mean come on! I almost wished I hadn't watched the last 2 eps ( i re-watch the series from time to time. ). Did anything actually occur in those last two? I mean they even didnt animate half of it, with shitty marker art .....

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:30 pm You do know I am talking about the orig ending, not End of Eva ? The Orig ending was just a bunch of sloppy put together dialog, although some of it interesting, was just stale dribble, reusing the same thoughts over and over..."is this me, am I me" etc etc etc. If you are talking about the orig ending, your one of the few so far that has preferred that ending vs. End of Eva......

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:35 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: If you are talking about the orig ending, your one of the few so far that has preferred that ending vs. End of Eva......

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:36 pm TROLL ALERT! DO NOT FEED!

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:45 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: your one of the few so far

[]: Re: Ending of original series             Quote Post by J_Faulkner » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:57 pm Professor Kaos, My heartiest welcome to you to this online Evangelion community. I must apologize on behalf of member skikes for his most rude remark towards you, and rest assured that I for one do not consider you a troll (such a vague and meaningless label to pigeon-hole people, no offence to pigeons). Your thoughts will be a valuable contribution to the continued evolution of Evangelion fandom. ProfessorKaos64 wrote: Some psychiatric simulator???

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Apr 17, 2010 8:59 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: You do know I am talking about the orig ending, not End of Eva ? The Orig ending was just a bunch of sloppy put together dialog, although some of it interesting, was just stale dribble, reusing the same thoughts over and over..."is this me, am I me" etc etc etc. If you are talking about the orig ending, your one of the few so far that has preferred that ending vs. End of Eva......

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sat Apr 17, 2010 9:09 pm EoTV = Completed subversion of narrative as persona/lie and themes as driving truth beneath it all Not liking EoTV = Reveals a person who prefers nice lies to the truth and superficial entertainment to ingenious art

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sat Apr 17, 2010 9:12 pm I can't say I prefer the TV ending of EoE, since so much would be missed by skipping the movie. And the animation, the score, and the editing of EoE is so beautiful. but on the other hand, I can't say that I prefer EoE over the TV ending, since so much insight into EoE, or even different perspectives of the ending, can be pulled from the TV ending. The two endings work best together. At first ep. 25 and 26 made one curious as to what EoE would so to explain/discover about the events within the ending. After watching EoE, the episodes seemed to give more insight into the minds of the characters during the events of EoE. I've yet to come across writings, other than perhaps scripture itself, that have that effect on the audience. (Not saying the Evangelion is "The Bible", but just that the writing styles seem to pull similar reactions. I've yet to actually watch just the originally aired episodes without the movie, and then the director's cut episodes with the movie, all within the separate contexts of each other. I think I'll make that my next goal with the Eva series.

[]: Re: Ending of original series             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Sat Apr 17, 2010 9:14 pm ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I apologize if there is already a thread, Im new, so you can laugh lol. In the orig. series, not end of eva which ruled, just what in the HELL were they trying to pass over our eyes in ep. 25/26 of the orig.????? Some psychiatric simulator??? I mean come on! I almost wished I hadn't watched the last 2 eps ( i re-watch the series from time to time. ). Did anything actually occur in those last two? I mean they even didnt animate half of it, with shitty marker art .....

[]:             Quote Post by BeckWreck » Sat Apr 17, 2010 11:15 pm It's one of those things that make you go 'WTF?' the first time, but then you read theories and dabble in some of Freud's works and go back to revisit it and... WTF. It makes sense if you consider EoE a complementation to EoTV, at least for me. I believe both happened but I am sure someone else will argue against that.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Apr 18, 2010 12:28 am I prefer the original ending to the remake of the sequel rather its later additional remake.

[]: Re: Ending of original series             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 18, 2010 2:09 am ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I apologize if there is already a thread

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Sun Apr 18, 2010 7:12 am Granted, the animation was fucking shit, but before I give you a definitive answer, what do you think happened that was important?:

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Apr 18, 2010 7:45 am ProfessorKaos64 wrote: I was wondering why they were shot...so Gendo explains why they were? I will have to rewatch them today.

[]:             Quote Post by ProfessorKaos64 » Sun Apr 18, 2010 8:08 am Is there a thread that explains "complementation " ? Sorry If I dont quite get it. Does it mean that one (the reality) can exist , if the other (non reality or "the instrumentality"), did not? Thanks for being kind to a newbie here

Article: Ending of original series
 17, 2010 9:12 pm I can't say I prefer the TV ending of EoE, since so much would be missed by skipping the movie. And the animation, the score, and the editing of EoE is so beautiful. but on the other hand, I can't say that I prefer EoE over the TV ending, since so much insight into EoE, or even different perspectives of the ending, can be pulled from the TV ending. The two endings work best together. At first ep. 25 and 26 made one curious as to what EoE would so to explain/discover about the events within the ending. After watching EoE, the episodes seemed to give more insight into the minds of the characters during the events of EoE. I've yet to come across writings, other than perhaps scripture itself, that have that effect on the audience. (Not saying the Evangelion is "The Bible", but just that the writing styles seem to pull similar reactions. I've yet to actually watch just the originally aired episodes without the movie, and then the director's cut episodes with the movie, all within the separate contexts of each other. I think I'll make that my next goal with the Eva series.
